    My Photography

    Exploring through my lenses, the beauty, Nature has endowed us with.

    My Instagram

    The dawning of an advanced civilization

    A picture is worth a thousand words.

    Pack your bags and walk the path less travelled

    On a fine morning, with smile on my face, I responded to The Mountain’s Call
    (a still from the end of the night trek)
    Location: Rajgad Fort (4,600 ft. AMSL), Maharashtra, India


    Kedareshwar Cave (4,665 ft. AMSL)
    It is said that the pillars were built to depict the four Yugas of Life - Satya Yuga, Tretha Yuga, Dwapara Yuga and Kali Yuga. When a Yuga comes to the end of its time, one of the pillars is said to break down. And exactly one pillar is left.
    Location: Harishchandragad, Maharashtra, India


    The labyrinth is not what you think. It is simply a rainwater harvesting/storage place. Even in summer, water remains cold.
    Location: Harishchandragad, Maharashtra, India


    When a journey crosses a milestone, relax, breathe, and rejuvenate. Even the setting Sun smilingly peeps and say, see you tomorrow.
    Location: Taramati peak, Harishchandragad, Maharashtra, India


    The woods are lovely, dark and deep,
    But I have promises to keep,
    And miles to go before I sleep,
    And miles to go before I sleep.
    - By Robert Frost
    Location: IIT Bombay, Mumbai, India


    Chand me daag hai, fir bhi khubsoorat hai.


    Leader crow addressing everybody: All are assigned their jobs now. Let’s move; Sun is coming up.


    After all the crows left, the tree was feeling lonely. But it is happy because it knows that it is going to see them again tomorrow dawn.

    Yin yang

    These slums play an important role in running Mumbai.
    कहते है - अमीरी का एहसास तभी होता है, जब गरीबी का अश्तित्व होता है
    English translation: You feel being rich only when you know the poor exists; You feel happy because you have felt the sorrow earlier.
    Location: from Sameer hill, Mumbai, India


    Creature of emotion, lost in his own dream.
    Location: Cherrapunji, Assam, India


    One of the Seven Sister Falls
    Location: Nohkalikai Falls, Cherrapunji, Assam, India


    I have never seen ‘those stems’ so big. Those stems are Dalchini (Cinnamon). And The bottles contain something else (honey :)).
    Location: Cherrapunji, Assam, India


    Automation Lab group
    Front row (from left): (Prof.) Sharad Bhartiya, Kannan M Moudgalya, Mani Bhushan, Ravindra D Gudi, and Sachin C Patwardhan
    Location: IIT Bombay, Mumbai, India


    Team Himachal
    Location: Himachal Pradesh, India


    The left one is a fan of Shahrukh Khan, and the right one is a fan of Superman…
    I presume.
    Location: Munnar, Kerala, India
